name: Project Initiation
goal: Understand hedl's distribution model, document Kypr's requirements, and define the phase 2 build scope. No skill code is written in this phase.
definition_of_done: docs/requirements.md exists, covering: what Kypr is, security model (git-crypt mandatory, no bypass), distribution (hedl model), agent compatibility (agent-agnostic core, thin harness wiring), workspace isolation (personal/professional), session model (interactive only, no background processes)
docs/hedl-study.md exists, documenting hedl's distribution model, directory structure, install mechanism, and constraints Kypr must replicate or diverge from
docs/adr/ADR-001-distribution-model.md records the decision to follow hedl exactly vs. adapt, with evidence from the hedl study
Phase 2 work items are drafted in .work/work.json covering the full skill/kypr/ build
CLAUDE.md exists at repo root with current phase and project context
am_i_done.py passes on a feature branch
constraints: No skill/kypr/ code written during phase 1 — scope and requirements only
Every design decision must reference docs/requirements.md or docs/hedl-study.md
H1 fix (check_checklist '(or N/A with reason)' gap from SEED-0001) must be resolved in the first PR of this phase
assumptions_to_validate: hedl's distribution model is documented well enough to replicate (study will confirm or flag gaps)
Kypr's scope fits within what hedl's skill model supports (agent-agnostic adapters are feasible)
learning_goals: What exactly does hedl's install mechanism do, and what must Kypr replicate?
What are the minimum workspace isolation requirements for personal/professional separation?
